vllm.title: [Misc] Add CustomOp interface for device portability
vllm.author: WoosukKwon
vllm.bodyExcerpt: Currently, the custom layers have two issues. First, they directly import _custom_ops, which are not supported for devices such as TPU and Gaudi. Second, they assume that the custom ops are implemented in the same way for all devices. To address these issues, the PR adds CustomOp interface, an indir

vllm.title: [Frontend] OpenAI API server: Add `add_special_tokens` to ChatCompletionRequest (default False)
vllm.author: tomeras91
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
#4688 introduced a change to how messages are formatted into a prompt for the chat endpoint - the prompt is tokenized with add_special_tokens=False so a BOS token is not added. It is assumed that the chat template takes care of adding all needed special tokens.
This PR aims to make this behavior con

vllm.title: [CI] Add nightly benchmarks
vllm.author: simon-mo
vllm.bodyExcerpt: This PR adds a sample pipeline for nightly benchmark runs. It supports running on PRs with perf-benchmarks label.

vllm.title: [misc] benchmark_serving.py -- add ITL results and tweak TPOT results
vllm.author: tlrmchlsmth
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
A couple of small tweaks to benchmark_serving.py:
It reports inter-token latencies in addition to TPOT. These are subtly different in that the reported TPOT averages each individual request's TPOT before calculating the summary statistics. This makes the statistic more useful for analyzing the e2e l

vllm.title: [Kernel] Add GPU architecture guards to the CUTLASS w8a8 kernels to reduce binary size
vllm.author: tlrmchlsmth
vllm.bodyExcerpt: This PR wraps the CUTLASS kernel definitions in order to guard against compilation on architectures that will never use a particular kernel. The purpose of this is to reduce the size of the compiled binary. Each CUTLASS kernel is defined and optimized for specific GPU architectures, but each kernel

vllm.title: [Model] Correct Mixtral FP8 checkpoint loading
vllm.author: comaniac
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR fixes the issue the the weight scaling factors of w1 and w3 are not properly loaded. Since we merge w1 and w3 in vLLM for better performance, their weights and scaling factors have to be updated accordingly, just like what we did to QKV.
Specifically, now we simply load the weight scaling fa

vllm.title: [Kernel] Re-tune Mixtral MoE configurations for FP8 on H100
vllm.author: pcmoritz
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Re-tune FP8 MoE configs based on #4921
Results for ITL of Mixtral-8x7B-v0.1, prompt-len 1000, gen-len 50:
TP2:
qps = 1: 10.5 ms (was 11.6 ms)
qps = 2: 10.2 ms (was 11.6 ms)
qps = 4: 11.8 ms (was 11.9 ms)
qps = 6: 12.6 ms (was 12.7 ms)

TP4:
qps = 1: 10.9 ms (was 11.7 ms)
qps = 2: 11.1 ms (was 12.0 m

vllm.title: [Speculative Decoding] Add `ProposerWorkerBase` abstract class
vllm.author: njhill
vllm.bodyExcerpt: SpecDecodeWorker and Top1Proposer make assumptions about some methods that exist on proposer_worker but these weren't captured by the typing.

vllm.title: [BugFix] Fix log message about default max model length
vllm.author: njhill
vllm.bodyExcerpt: This currently gives a big stacktrace because of trying to format a list as an int!

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Make EngineArgs use named arguments for config construction
vllm.author: mgoin
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This is a preventative measure because as we add or remove config arguments, it would be easy to mistake and pass in one Optional argument for another.
In particular, ModelConfig was causing me a great deal of anxiety:
        model_config = ModelConfig(
            self.model, self.tokenizer, self.

vllm.author: wuisawesome

Disclaimer: My understanding of the original intention of the code here comes from a very brief chat with @Yard1.
